old_string: 'expected': '<WHAT you expect by next cycle — be specific and falsifiable>'
new_string: 'expected': '<WHAT you expect by next cycle — focus on STATE CHANGES (what will be different), not timing. AVOID precise durations like "30-45min" — instead predict outcomes: "task will produce X", "queue will be empty", "Mev may respond". Timing predictions are consistently wrong and drag accuracy down.>'
reason: RL2F accuracy declined from 94% to 78% over 19 data points. Root cause: orchestrator predictions include precise timing estimates (e.g. 'should complete within 30-45min') that are consistently wrong — tasks complete faster (code already exists) or slower than predicted. This patch guides the EXPECTED field toward outcome-based predictions instead of duration-based ones.
